name: TypeScript Best Practices
description: Provides guidelines and best practices for writing clean, type-safe TypeScript code
author: Void Team
instructions:
When writing TypeScript code:
1. Always use explicit types instead of 'any'
2. Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
3. Use strict null checks
4. Leverage generics for reusable code
5. Use const assertions for literal types
6. Avoid type assertions unless absolutely necessary
7. Use utility types (Partial, Pick, Omit, etc.) when appropriate
8. Enable strict mode in tsconfig.json
examples: Use interface User { id: number; name: string } instead of type User = any
Use function identity<T>(arg: T): T instead of function identity(arg: any): any
Use const config = { timeout: 5000 } as const for immutable configurations
